three_body.instability_note: Low instability — ESG score, sector position, and FTSE 100 rank tell a broadly consistent story (21-pt spread).
three_body.resolution: Our resolution: Integrity 56/100 × WEM 35/100 → 'Engagement Priority'. Jangani, Date & Tucker (SSRN 5618192, 2026) show driver rankings shift across regime windows (COVID → COP26 → energy crisis). We anchor to verifiable harm metrics (emissions, pay ratios, enforcement records) that are regime-stable — not ESG labels that oscillate with market narrative.

summary_investor: Amazon.com Inc is projected to hold steady in its energy peer ranking (5→5 of 8) over 3 months. FTSE 100 position: 23rd→24th percentile. WEM 35/100 — material ESG risk — recommend underweight or significant risk premium. Placebo Index 0.76 — ESG narrative overstates real performance.
summary_corporate: 12-month sector ranking forecast: hold steady (5→5 of 8). FTSE 100: 23rd→24th percentile. Highest-leverage action: Business Model Resilience (77% materiality) — this single driver has the greatest impact on FTSE 100 ranking improvement.
summary_auditor: Of 2 ESG claims analysed, 0 (0%) are independently verifiable. 1 claim(s) directly contradicted by external data — moderate restatement risk. ESG provider spread: 12 pts — significant methodological disagreement reflected in Integrity Score. Priority: third-party audit of Business Model Resilience measurement methodology.
crowd_consensus: Aggregated signal across investor, corporate, and audit lenses: Lagging — underweight; high harm + ESG overstatement confirmed. Driver rankings are temporally unstable across market regimes (Jangani et al., SSRN 5618192). The 3-body instability — ESG score, peer rank, FTSE position pulling in different directions — is resolved by WEM (35/100) × Integrity (56/100): regime-stable signals grounded in verifiable harm, not rater methodology.
